skill.name: remove-git-worktree
skill.description: git worktreeを削除します。間違えて作成した場合や、作業完了後のクリーンアップに使用します。
skill.summary: git worktreeを削除します。間違えて作成した場合や、作業完了後のクリーンアップに使用します。
skill.author: AKABANAKK

security_audit.summary: The static shell and filesystem findings are mostly legitimate for a Git worktree cleanup skill, and no malicious intent or prompt injection was found. Risk remains medium because the script removes a computed filesystem path and force-deletes a local branch based on user input.

medium_findings.title: Irreversible Git Cleanup From User Input
medium_findings.description: The script accepts a branch name argument, removes the matching worktree, and force-deletes the local branch. This is intended behavior, but it can cause local data loss if the wrong branch name is supplied.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly maps user input to the target worktree and then calls git worktree remove and git branch -D. The behavior is explicit and legitimate, but the destructive effect is clear.

medium_findings.title: Git Option Injection Risk
medium_findings.description: The branch name is passed to git branch -D without an option delimiter. A branch-like value beginning with a hyphen could be interpreted as a command option instead of a branch name.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command uses quoted input, which prevents shell injection, but it does not use -- before the branch name. This is a known command option handling concern for user-controlled arguments.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Documentation and Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The documentation command example and the shell shebang are expected for a skill that runs a local helper script. The weak cryptography alert on the description line has no supporting evidence in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a Japanese description, a shell usage example, and a standard shebang. I found no cryptographic algorithm or instruction to bypass security review.

low_findings.title: Fixed Parent Directory Path Is Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The parent directory marker is part of a fixed sibling worktree layout. The branch name is quoted and slashes are replaced before composing the worktree path, so direct path traversal was not found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static traversal signal is explained by a literal ../ segment used to reach a sibling directory. The script still performs filesystem deletion, but the specific traversal finding is not supported.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Executes Git Destructive Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill invokes a Bash script that runs git worktree remove and git branch -D. These commands are powerful local operations and should be used only after the target branch is verified.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive Git commands are present in the script and match the skill purpose. There is no evidence of malware, but the local impact is significant.

content.value_statement: Stale or mistaken Git worktrees can clutter a repository and leave unused local branches behind. This skill provides a focused cleanup command for removing a named worktree and its local branch.
actual_capabilities: Runs a local Bash script to remove a Git worktree by branch name.
Converts branch slashes into hyphens for the expected worktree folder name.
Finds the current repository root with git rev-parse.
Checks that the computed worktree directory exists before removal.
Deletes the matching local branch when it exists.
limitations: Only supports the repository layout expected by the script.
Does not confirm the target before running destructive Git commands.
Does not protect unmerged or unpushed local branch work.
Requires Bash and Git to be available in the local environment.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Finished Feature Work
use_cases.description: Remove a completed feature worktree and delete the related local branch after review or merge.

use_cases.title: Undo an Accidental Worktree
use_cases.description: Remove a worktree that was created for the wrong branch or task.

use_cases.title: Standardize Repository Maintenance
use_cases.description: Use one repeatable command for local worktree cleanup across a team repository.

prompt_templates.title: Remove One Worktree
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the Git worktree for branch feature/example using this skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner cleanup for a known branch

prompt_templates.title: Check Before Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the worktree path that would be removed for branch feature/example, then run the cleanup if it exists.

prompt_templates.title: Clean Up After Merge
prompt_templates.prompt: After confirming branch feature/example is merged or no longer needed, remove its worktree and local branch.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Worktree Removal Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the removal command for branch feature/example and explain any local data loss risk before executing it.

output_examples.input: Remove the worktree for branch feature/new-dashboard.
output_examples.output: The skill reports the target branch, shows the computed worktree path, removes the worktree, deletes the local branch if present, and finishes with a completion message.

output_examples.input: Remove a worktree for a branch that has no matching directory.
output_examples.output: The skill reports that the expected worktree directory was not found and stops without deleting a branch.

output_examples.input: Run cleanup without providing a branch name.
output_examples.output: The skill prints usage guidance and exits without making repository changes.

best_practices: Confirm the target branch name before running the cleanup command.
Commit, stash, or back up important work before removing a worktree.
Use the skill only from inside the repository that owns the worktree.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill on a branch with unreviewed local work.
Do not use untrusted branch names without checking the command target.
Do not assume it works for every custom worktree directory layout.

faq.question: What does this skill remove?
faq.answer: It removes the computed Git worktree directory and deletes the matching local branch when that branch exists.

faq.question: Does it remove remote branches?
faq.answer: No. The script only deletes a local branch with git branch -D.

faq.question: Can it delete uncommitted work?
faq.answer: It can remove a worktree path. Users should save or back up work before running it.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked medium risk?
faq.answer: It performs destructive local Git and filesystem operations from a user-supplied branch name.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
